utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: eugene walker , n- date of birth 4/14/1960 . he's a 61-year-old male who presents today , uh , for a routine follow-up with chronic medical conditions .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: of note , the patient underwent an aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair on 1/22/2013 . regarding his blood work from 4/10/2021 , the patient's alkaline phosphatate- phosphatase , excuse me , was elevated to 156 . his lipid panel showed elevated total cholesterol of 247 , hdl of 66 , ldl of 166 , and triglycerides at 74 . the patient's tsh was normal at 2.68 . his cbc was unremarkable . his most recent vitamin d level was at the high end of normal at 94 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good morning , mr. walker . how are you doing ? i mean , it's been a crazy year .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing fine , for the most part , but there are a few things i want to cover today .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure . go right ahead .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , well , i'm having more fatigue , but i do n't know if it's age or if it's just , you know , drained at the end of the day . but i still ride my bike . i ca n't go as fast as i used to . i'm still riding , and , you know , after a long bike ride , i'll sit down and then boom . i'm out , you know ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . what's a long bike ride to you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , 20 to 30 miles .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: 20 to 30 miles on a road bike ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , road bike . i think it's a time thing . if i had more time , i would try to do my 40 miles , but i have n't done that . obviously , we're too early in the season so my typical ride is , like , 20 , 30 . in years back , i could do 40 on a good day . i can still do 20 but , you know , i'm tired and have to take a break when i get home .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i understand .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and tyler's my buddy . he's always nice and waits for me , but i used to be able to beat him . but now , he waits for me all the time . he's older than me and it- it kills me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i can imagine that would upset me too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , the last time , you know , you found a heart thing , then . just making sure that the valve is holding out , you know ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right . so , when was your last stress test ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it was september 9th , 2019 , because i'm eight years out from surgery , and back then , they said , you know , it's going to last eight years . and i'm at that year , so i just want to make sure . i asked dr. lewis for an echocardiogram to see how i'm doing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but it's not ... like , nothing has changed drastically since i saw you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . do you still go down to hopkins at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not at all . i just get follow-ups intermittently , here . going there is just ... it's too much stress .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: one more thing , i want to make sure i do n't forget . my wife and friends tell me that when i walk , i purse my lips when i'm breathing . other doctors have said , " did you notice your pursed lips breathing ? " i do n't know if that's a bad habit or what .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . is there any wheezing associated with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no wheezing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you're able to bike 30 miles and mostly keep up with your friend , tyler , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . the only other thing i want to mention is it's not like i do routine testicular exams , but i know i have this little nodule on my right testicle .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on the testicle or the epididymis ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: epididymis . uh , i really do n't know . i'm not super concerned . i read a little online . just wanted to ask you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and did you have a vasectomy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . let me pull my notes out and make sure i mentioned everything i wanted to tell you . those were the only things and it's not like my tiredness is depression or anything . i'm a pretty happy guy overall , you know ? i just know you would ask those questions .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what time are you going to sleep , and about how many hours do you sleep a night ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it varies . usually , i get six to seven hours of sleep . i get out of bed some days to be at work by 7:00 , lecture , and i try to work out in the morning . i- i do n't ... i'm not ... i'm not always successful , and now what i do is i- i do make reservations twice a week for a 5:45 swim in the morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so you're getting six to seven hours of sleep , and has your wife ever mentioned if you snore or stop breathing at any time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i believe i snore a little bit , but she's never said anything about me not breathing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , you're currently taking vitamin d3 , around 5000 units on sundays , and two thous ... or 50,000 units on sundays , excuse me , and 2000s on the other six days , and then clindamycin prior to dental procedures , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you had a covid-19 shot yet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've received both . my first dose on january 15th , '21 and my second on february 5th , '21 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . if you'd hop up here on the table , we're just going to do a physical exam .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , mr. walker , overall you're doing well . i'm going to order an echocardiogram and a stress test . i also recommend that you follow up with cardiology , i think dr. vincent sanchez would be a great fit for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: also your recent labs showed an elevated alkaline phosphatase level at 156 . now this could be related to your liver but most likely related to your bone health . we're going to check a few labs today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you're going to have them done today ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , sir , and we will send the results through your patient portal unless something is way off then we'll give you a call .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now as far as your breathing , i observed the pursed lip breathing and your exhalation is low . i think you should do a pulmonary function test to further evaluate , and i'll order that as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now the nodule in your right testicle should be evaluated by urology , and we will place that referral today , also .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds like i'm going to be busy getting this all checked out .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , sir . now you are due for your mmr and i'm recommend you get the shingles vaccine as well . you have completed your covid-19 , so that's good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now i'm going to have , uh ... have you return in about a year for your wellness visit . we'll see you back sooner if needed after i review all those labs and those other studies .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any other questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , doc . i think you covered it all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . okay , the nurse will , uh , be back in a minute to give you mmr today , and the front desk will line up a time to do the shingles vaccine next month .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thanks , doc . have a great day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , i used my general physical exam template for respiratory notate : pursed lip breathing , low exhalation phase , clear to oscillation , no wheezing . uh , genitalia notate : right testicle with two to three millimeters palpable nodule does not feel as if it will ... does not feel as if with the epididymis or variococele ; left testicle , normal ; no hernia . all other portions of the physical exam are normal default .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: assessment history of the aortic aneurysm repair : the patient underwent and aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair on 1/20/2013 . he is doing well overall and currently asymptomatic . he is currently not seen by cardiology routinely . suggest the following up and suggested vincent sanchez as his physician . we will perform an echocardiogram , eh , slash , stress test .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: elevated alkaline phosphatase level . most recent cmd showed elevation at 156 . this could be related to his liver but most likely re- related to his bone health . i've ordered an alkaline phosphatase and again a gt .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lung field abnormal finding on exona ... excuse me , on examination . the patient has been noted to purse his lips while breathing . he was found himself ... he has found himself feeling more fatigued at the end of the day . he does bicycle around 20 to 30 miles at a time . his exhal- exhalation phase is low on exam , and i've ordered pfts today to further ... for further evaluation .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: the right testicular nodule is about two to three millimeters , i've noted on the exam . there's no hernia palpable , and i have suggested reaching out to ro ... urology for a possible ultrasound .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: preventative health : the most recent blood work was reviewed with no significant abnormalaries ... abnormalities other than the cmv . uh , we will perform mmr titer today . i have suggested the shingles vaccine and he is fully vaccinated against covid-19 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: patient will return , uh , for a follow-up in one year for a wellness visit , sooner if needed . he is to call with any questions or concerns .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 61-year-old male presenting for a routine follow-up of chronic medical conditions.
The patient underwent aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair on 01/22/2013.
Recent lab results show elevated alkaline phosphatase at 156, total cholesterol 247, HDL 66, LDL 166, triglycerides 74, TSH 2.68, and vitamin D at 94.
Fatigue
The patient reports increased fatigue and difficulty maintaining previous cycling speeds.
The doctor asks the patient to define the distance of his bike rides.
The patient cycles 20 to 30 miles per ride.
Cardiovascular health
The doctor asks when the patient's last stress test was performed.
The patient's last stress test was on 09/09/2019 and he is requesting an echocardiogram.
Pursed lip breathing
The doctor asks if there is any wheezing associated with the patient's pursed lip breathing.
The patient denies any wheezing.
Testicular nodule
The patient reports a nodule on his right testicle.
The doctor asks if the nodule is on the testicle or the epididymis.
The patient is unsure if the nodule is on the testicle or epididymis.
The doctor asks if the patient has had a vasectomy.
The patient denies having a vasectomy.
The doctor asks about the patient's sleep schedule and duration.
The patient gets 6 to 7 hours of sleep per night.
The doctor asks if the patient snores or has signs of sleep apnea.
The patient reports snoring but denies apnea.
The patient takes vitamin D3 (50,000 IU on Sundays and 2,000 IU daily) and clindamycin prior to dental procedures.
The doctor asks if the patient has received the COVID-19 vaccine.
The patient received COVID-19 vaccine doses on 01/15/2021 and 02/05/2021.
The doctor orders an echocardiogram and stress test and recommends follow-up with Dr. Vincent Sanchez.
The doctor orders alkaline phosphatase and gamma GT testing.
The doctor notes pursed lip breathing and low exhalation phase on exam.
The doctor orders pulmonary function testing.
The doctor places a referral to urology for the testicular nodule.
The doctor recommends the shingles vaccine and performs an MMR titer.
The patient is to return in one year for a wellness visit.

case_description: follow up visit of chronic medical conditions; fatigue; pursed lips breathing; nodule on epididymis
